verificationStatus: not-verified — this risk register contains constructed-indicative risks illustrative of typical capital market technology risk concerns under SC-GL/6-2023; specific risk ratings and treatment plans are examples and must be tailored
title: Capital Market Technology Risk Register
description: Indicative risk register for SC-regulated capital market intermediaries and operators under SC-GL/6-2023 Guidelines on Technology Risk Management.

risks.title: Trading System Outage During Market Hours
risks.description: Critical trading systems experience unplanned outage during market hours, preventing the firm from executing client orders and managing positions, resulting in financial losses and regulatory scrutiny.
existingControls: Redundant trading infrastructure with automatic failover
Capacity management with headroom for peak volumes
risks.treatmentPlan: Implement active-active trading infrastructure; conduct quarterly failover testing; establish alternative trading channels; define market-hours incident response SLAs; maintain direct market access backup.

risks.title: Cyber Attack on Market Infrastructure
risks.description: Sophisticated cyber attack targeting capital market infrastructure (trading systems, settlement systems, or market data feeds) causing disruption to market operations and potential data breach.
existingControls: Advanced threat detection for trading infrastructure
risks.treatmentPlan: Deploy advanced threat hunting for market-specific attack vectors; implement zero-trust architecture for trading networks; participate in SC and industry threat intelligence sharing; conduct annual red team exercise targeting trading systems.

risks.title: Market Data Feed Corruption
risks.description: Market data feeds are corrupted or delayed, causing incorrect pricing, erroneous trade executions, or compliance failures in best execution obligations.
existingControls: Multiple data feed sources for critical data
risks.treatmentPlan: Implement real-time market data validation with anomaly detection; maintain redundant data feeds from independent sources; establish data correction and trade cancellation procedures; conduct regular data integrity testing.

risks.title: Algorithmic Trading Malfunction
risks.description: An algorithmic trading system malfunctions, generating erroneous orders at high speed, potentially causing market disruption, significant financial losses, and regulatory action.
existingControls: Pre-trade risk checks with order size and price limits
Kill switch capability for algorithm halt
risks.treatmentPlan: Enhance pre-trade risk controls with pattern-based detection; implement automated kill switch with sub-second response; require comprehensive testing including stress and edge-case scenarios; deploy real-time algorithm monitoring with anomaly alerting.

risks.title: Settlement System Failure
risks.description: Settlement processing systems fail, causing delays in trade settlement, potential fails-to-deliver, counterparty exposure, and regulatory reporting failures.
risks.treatmentPlan: Test manual settlement procedures quarterly; maintain updated contact protocols with Bursa Malaysia Depository; participate in industry-wide DR exercises; ensure settlement system in scope of annual DR testing.

risks.title: Client Data Breach
risks.description: Unauthorised access or exfiltration of client personal and financial data held by the capital market intermediary, resulting in PDPA violations, SC enforcement action, and reputational damage.
existingControls: Data encryption at rest and in transit
risks.treatmentPlan: Implement zero-trust data access model; deploy advanced DLP with ML-based detection; conduct regular data protection impact assessments; enhance monitoring of privileged access to client data systems.

risks.title: Non-Compliance with SC-GL/6-2023
risks.description: The entity fails to meet SC-GL/6-2023 requirements during regulatory examination, resulting in enforcement action, remediation orders, or reputational damage.
existingControls: Internal audit coverage of technology risk
risks.treatmentPlan: Conduct biannual self-assessment against SC-GL/6-2023; maintain standing evidence library; ensure technology risk in scope of annual internal audit; track and close prior examination findings promptly.

risks.title: Critical Vendor Failure Impacting Market Operations
risks.description: A critical third-party technology vendor experiences operational failure or insolvency, disrupting the firm's ability to operate trading, clearing, or settlement systems.
existingControls: Vendor risk tiering with enhanced monitoring for critical vendors
risks.treatmentPlan: Maintain alternative vendor options for critical services; test vendor exit plans biennially; monitor vendor financial health continuously; include vendor failure scenarios in BCP testing.

risks.title: AI Model Producing Biased or Erroneous Recommendations
risks.description: AI/ML models used in trading, portfolio management, or compliance produce biased or erroneous outputs leading to poor client outcomes, regulatory non-compliance, or financial losses.
risks.treatmentPlan: Implement model risk management framework for AI/ML; conduct bias testing and fairness assessments; require human-in-the-loop for material AI-driven decisions; maintain model inventory with governance documentation.

risks.title: Ransomware Attack on Back-Office Systems
risks.description: Ransomware attack encrypts back-office systems including compliance, reporting, and client management platforms, disrupting regulatory reporting and client servicing.
existingControls: Offline backup strategy with regular testing
risks.treatmentPlan: Transfer residual financial impact through cyber insurance covering ransomware, business interruption, and regulatory fines. Retain technical controls (immutable backups, application whitelisting, EDR). Test backup restoration quarterly. Insurance policy must cover SC notification obligations.
